Christ himself is the foundation of the church, which God has laid. Holiness is the strength and firmness of the church. Let us not be ashamed of the church of Christ in its humblest condition, nor of those who belong to it, since such glorious things are spoken of it. No one can lay another foundation than that which is laid, even Jesus Christ. The glorious things spoken of Zion by the Spirit were all typical of Christ and his work and offices; of the gospel church, its privileges and members; of heaven, its glory and perfect happiness.

The church of Christ is more glorious and excellent than the nations of the earth. In the records of heaven, the most humble of those who are born again stand registered. When God renders to every man according to his works, he will observe who enjoyed the privileges of his sanctuary. Much was given to them, and much will be required of them. Let those who dwell in Zion mark this and live up to their profession. Zion's songs shall be sung with joy and triumph. The springs of the joy of a worldly person are in wealth and pleasure; but of a gracious soul, in the word of God and prayer. All grace and consolation are derived from Christ, through his ordinances, to the souls of believers.
